# Taylor (NotGreg) — Opportunities & Considerations — February 17, 2026

---

## Opportunities Proposed by Taylor

### 1. Payment Processing Referral Income
Taylor pays Dax lifetime residuals if Dax refers business owners to him and Taylor closes the deal. A student at UGA reportedly earns ~$12,000/month this way. No minimum commitment — just introductions.

**Dax's read:** Interesting in concept. Defer to a dedicated conversation. Requires credibility research first.

---

### 2. Stripe → NMI/Authorized.net Replacement
Taylor wants to replace DD's Stripe payment gateway with his processing system.

**Dax's read:** High friction. DD has custom-built everything with APIs and integrations. Not a decision Dax can make unilaterally — requires developer (Zach) input. Probably a no.

---

### 3. Discord Building Exchange
Taylor wants Dax to advise on structuring and monetizing his Discord community. In return, Taylor offers free business/banking counsel (no cost).

**Dax's read:** Easy to do. Dax is willing to help. Low time commitment — screen share, give some feedback, minimal obligation.

---

### 4. Live Music Discord Thread
Taylor is building a Discord for live concerts/music/art. Wants to potentially thread concert announcements into DD when his app goes live.

**Dax's read:** No action needed now. Could revisit if Taylor's app actually launches and has an audience worth threading.

---

### 5. Business / Banking Counsel
Taylor offers himself as a free banking advisor — business accounts, lending, credit structure, operational finance.

**Dax's read:** Potentially useful down the line as DD grows. Not urgent now.

---

## Risks / Watch-Outs

- **Credibility unverified.** Taylor cold DM'd Dax with a lot of offers. Wait for him to send over company links and verify before committing to anything.
- **Bookmaking angle.** Taylor brought up a private bookie model and offered to set Dax up with a portfolio of bettors. Dax declined. This topic is closed.
- **Stripe pitch.** If Taylor pushes the Stripe replacement again, the answer is no until/unless Zach weighs in separately.
